data.title: Analyst Maintains Hold on Aston Martin as Improving Fundamentals Offset by Ongoing Execution Risks and Limited Upside
data.description:
In a report released today, Christian Frenes from Goldman Sachs reiterated a Hold rating on Aston Martin Lagonda Global Holdings plc, with a price target of p50.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Christian Frenes has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, including a balance between Aston Martin’s improving fundamentals and the risks still embedded in the story. While the company is making progress on operational execution and has opportunities from new model launches and brand strength, these positives are largely reflected in the current share price, limiting near-term upside.

At the same time, Christian notes that Aston Martin continues to face execution risk around margin improvement, volume growth, and deleveraging, all of which depend on consistent delivery over multiple years. Given this risk-reward profile, he judges that the stock does not yet offer a sufficiently compelling valuation discount or catalyst set to justify a more positive stance, leading him to recommend investors maintain positions rather than add meaningfully at this stage.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Unilever: Sell Rating Maintained as Macro Risks and Margin Uncertainty Persist; $4300 Price Target Unchanged
data.description:
Analyst David Hayes from Jefferies maintained a Sell rating on Unilever and keeping the price target at p4,300.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

David Hayes has given his Sell rating due to a combination of factors, despite acknowledging some positive elements in Unilever’s latest quarter. He views the stronger-than-expected volume and mix, particularly the notable volume rebound in HomeCare driven by Latin America, Brazil, and India, as a constructive start rather than a decisive turnaround.

However, he believes that maintaining guidance in the face of ongoing macroeconomic headwinds leaves meaningful downside risk, especially around margin execution. In his view, the share price is likely to benefit near term from outperforming peers on this print, but the heavy reliance on performance delivery and unresolved uncertainties, including the timing of the MKC transaction closure, justify keeping a cautious Sell stance, with the price target unchanged at $4300.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Maintains Hold as Cautious Capital Allocation and Rising Lease Exposure Cloud FY27 Outlook
data.description:
Whitbread, the Consumer Cyclical sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Simon LeChipre from Jefferies maintained a Hold rating on the stock and has a £21.00 price target.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Simon LeChipre has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, starting with a mixed overall update that points to likely cuts in market expectations for FY27. Management’s decision to maintain its five-year capital return goal, but to finance it via lower expansion spending and additional sale-and-leaseback transactions while suspending share buybacks in FY27, signals a more cautious capital allocation stance.

He also highlights that the planned increase in leasehold exposure will change the group’s risk and cash‑flow profile in a way investors will scrutinize. In addition, a soft consumer environment in the U.K. and persistent cost inflation suggest that current earnings forecasts may prove too optimistic, limiting near‑term upside for the shares and justifying a neutral, Hold recommendation.

In another report released on April 28, Barclays also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a £25.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Reiterates Buy on Amerigo Resources, Citing Strong Cash Generation and Leverage to Copper Prices with Unchanged Target Price
data.description:
Analyst Ben Pirie of Atrium Research maintained a Buy rating on Amerigo Resources, with a price target of C$8.50.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ben Pirie has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors including Amerigo’s substantial earnings leverage to copper prices and its strong first‑quarter performance. A relatively modest year‑over‑year increase in copper prices translated into more than a doubling of EBITDA, with margins reaching around half of revenue, underscoring a highly cash‑generative business model.

Amerigo also produced solid free cash flow, ended the quarter with a sizeable cash balance and no debt, and returned significant capital to shareholders through dividends and buybacks. With management emphasizing strong production, lower unit costs, and favourable copper market dynamics, Pirie believes the company is well positioned to continue generating robust cash flows, supporting his Buy rating and unchanged target price.

According to TipRanks, Pirie is ranked #55 out of 12163 analysts.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Maintains Buy on Organto After Near-Tripling 2025 Revenue, Strong Margin Outperformance and Reiterated $1.25 Price Target
data.description:
Nicholas Cortellucci, an analyst from Atrium Research, maintained the Buy rating on Organto Foods. The associated price target is C$1.25.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Nicholas Cortellucci has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, most notably Organto’s exceptional top-line expansion and consistent outperformance versus his forecasts. The company delivered nearly triple-digit annual revenue growth in 2025, with record sales and strong momentum exiting Q4, driven largely by higher volumes in key organic and fair-trade product lines.

At the same time, Organto’s gross margins exceeded expectations both for the year and the quarter, signaling improving operational efficiency even as the business scales rapidly. While EBITDA was slightly below his target, Cortellucci views the company as firmly on a path toward profitability in 2026, which, combined with its current valuation, supports maintaining a Buy recommendation and a $1.25 per share target price.

According to TipRanks, Cortellucci is ranked #11030 out of 12163 analysts.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
